metadata.title: The Schrödinger Equation
metadata.notes: Film five — the one the wave-function outro promised. Tangent EARLY (the equation IS the film; matter-waves precedent). THE visual: the clock and the shadow (B05, the chapter's own metaphor). B09 Fourier 1822 is the series' second DOCUMENT·annotate plane beat. Scope: what the equation says, stationarity, sloshing — separation algebra and the well belong to ch 5's film. TTS psi → 'sigh' from the start (film-four lesson).
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: In the first six months of 1926, an Austrian physicist published four papers that rebuilt physics. At the center of all four sat one equation.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In the first six months of nineteen twenty-six, an Austrian physicist published four papers that rebuilt physics. At the center of all four sat one equation.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Schrödinger (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Erwin Schrödinger 1926 at a cluttered Zurich desk, manuscripts; set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Last film: psi squared gives the odds. This film: the equation that moves psi. Schrödinger's equation — the law of motion for the quantum world.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Last film: sigh squared gives the odds. This film: the equation that moves sigh. Schrödinger's equation — the law of motion for the quantum world.

beats.narration_text: The left side: how the wave function is changing, right now. The right side: the energy operator's verdict on the state. Energy drives all change.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The left side: how the wave function is changing, right now. The right side: the energy operator's verdict on the state. Energy drives all change.

tangent.lhs: How the wave function is changing, right now.
tangent.rhs: The energy operator's verdict on the state.
tangent.claim: Energy is the engine of all quantum change.

glossary.mean: i turns change into rotation, not decay; ℏ sets the clock speed
measures total energy — kinetic plus potential

example.scenario: Separate space from time: each energy state gets a phase clock e^(−iEt/ℏ).
example.verdict: the clock spins — the shadow stands still
example.cost: Miss the phase and you miss interference — everything quantum.

beats.narration_text: i makes change into rotation, not decay. H-bar sets the clock speed. And the Hamiltonian — the energy operator — decides how fast each piece spins.
beats.tts_normalized_text: i makes change into rotation, not decay. H-bar sets the clock speed. And the Hamiltonian — the energy operator — decides how fast each piece spins.

beats.narration_text: Separate space from time: every energy state becomes a spinning phase, turning at energy over h-bar. One electron-volt: two hundred forty trillion turns a second.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Separate space from time: every energy state becomes a spinning phase, turning at energy over h-bar. One electron-volt: two hundred forty trillion turns a second.

beats.narration_text: The trick is one guess: maybe space and time separate. They do. The equation splits — a trivial clock in time, an eigenvalue problem in space.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The trick is one guess: maybe space and time separate. They do. The equation splits — a trivial clock in time, an eigenvalue problem in space.

viz.data: Fig 4.1 as motion: TDSE card at top; the ansatz Ψ = ψ(x)·φ(t) chip; equation forks into two branches — left card 'time: e^(−iEt/ℏ)' (trivial clock, terra), right card 'space: Ĥψ = Eψ' (the real problem, navy); E chip sits at the fork point
viz.note: The separation constant E is the hinge — label it as 'the same constant on both sides'

beats.narration_text: Each energy state is a clock: real part, imaginary part, spinning together. The probability is the clock's shadow. The clock spins. The shadow never moves.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Each energy state is a clock: real part, imaginary part, spinning together. The probability is the clock's shadow. The clock spins. The shadow never moves.

viz.data: THE visual: left — phasor circle, hand spinning at E/ℏ, Re (terra) and Im (gray dashed) components oscillating 90° apart beneath; right — |Ψ|² (navy filled hump) with a FROZEN chip; the left side animates the whole beat, the right side never moves
viz.note: Chapter's own metaphor, near-verbatim: 'the wave function is a clock; the probability density is the clock's shadow'

beats.narration_text: You've seen this physics before — inside every laser. Two mirrors, and only the waves that fit exactly between them survive. Everything else destroys itself.
beats.tts_normalized_text: You've seen this physics before — inside every laser. Two mirrors, and only the waves that fit exactly between them survive. Everything else destroys itself.

shot.note: AI-generated laser-cavity plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO·ai] open He-Ne laser cavity on an optical bench, two facing mirrors, one red standing beam (PROMPT LAW prompt in SHOTLIST); set shot.focus on the beam between the mirrors

beats.narration_text: Confinement plus wave equation equals a menu. Only certain shapes fit the walls; each shape carries its own energy. That menu is quantization.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Confinement plus wave equation equals a menu. Only certain shapes fit the walls; each shape carries its own energy. That menu is quantization.

viz.data: Two walls (ink verticals); standing modes n=1,2,3 draw on between them (navy), each with an energy chip E₁, E₂, E₃ climbing a small level ladder at right; a wrong-fit wave (crimson) enters, mismatches the wall, and fades — 'doesn't fit, doesn't exist'
viz.note: The cavity analogy made quantitative; sets up ch 5's well without doing its math

beats.narration_text: Mix two states and the spell breaks: the shadow moves. The blend sloshes at the beat between their energies — while the total energy holds perfectly still.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Mix two states and the spell breaks: the shadow moves. The blend sloshes at the beat between their energies — while the total energy holds perfectly still.

viz.data: Well with |Ψ|² for an equal 1+2 superposition sloshing left-right at the beat frequency (animate the cos((E₂−E₁)t/ℏ) cross term); beneath, a flat ⟨E⟩ line (crimson) with chip 'energy: constant' — the slosh above, the flatline below
viz.note: Chapter: |Ψ|² cross term at ω=(E₂−E₁)/ℏ; ⟨H⟩ = Σ|cₙ|²Eₙ exactly time-independent

beats.narration_text: And the mathematics? A century old. Fourier proved these building blocks complete in 1822 — for heat flow. Quantum mechanics is old mathematics in new clothes.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And the mathematics? A century old. Fourier proved these building blocks complete in eighteen twenty-two — for heat flow. Quantum mechanics is old mathematics in new clothes.
beats.new_visual_element: [SCAN] Fourier, Théorie analytique de la chaleur (1822), title page — archive.org/Gallica, PD. REMOTION PLANE: highlight sweep on the title keyed to 'Fourier', ring on '1822' keyed to 'eighteen twenty-two', gentle push. Degrades to hold+slow-zoom. Regions in annotations.json are needs_review until the scan is in hand.

beats.narration_text: Who was Erwin Schrödinger? He found the equation in six months, and spent decades uneasy about what it meant. His doubt became physics' most famous cat.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Who was Erwin Schrödinger? He found the equation in six months, and spent decades uneasy about what it meant. His doubt became physics' most famous cat.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Schrödinger (PORTRAIT preset, direct gaze) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Schrödinger kicker portrait, direct gaze; kicker typography (name · 1887–1961 · the one line) on the Remotion plane; degrades to serif label

beats.narration_text: Next: the simplest quantum system there is — a particle in a box — and the notes it's allowed to play.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: the simplest quantum system there is — a particle in a box — and the notes it's allowed to play.
